Module 4:
Launch List Secrets, Part 2
!
Executive Summary
“Launch List” defined: a list that you build for (and use during) a launch.
• 	Now to make a simple example, let’s assume you already have a list:
• 	They’re opted in to this list
• 	Now you’re going into a launch, so you create a launch list
• 	You create this other list and set it up through your list host
• 	To start your launch you send your main list into a squeeze page -
they’ll see a video training for your launch (PLC)
• 	When they opt in they get to see your PLC’s and eventually your
sales video/letter
So, ultimately, why are we sending people to another squeeze page and
asking them to opt in again? Because when you go into a launch the offer
is different from what they initially signed up for - you’re also going to be
emailing them a lot more.
If you add up all the email you’ll be sending during the launch it’s between
10-15 emails. By putting them through the process of joining your launch
list they’ll be interested and not dislike getting your emails.
!

-- 1 of 2 --

Also, if your prelaunch content is great they’ll love you by the end. Don’t be
afraid of sending people a lot of email, especially if they’ve asked for it by
opting in to your launch list.
The people that do opt in are so much warmer - it rejuvenates them and
creates recency. Is it possible to skip this and just send PLC to your main
list? Yes, you absolutely can.
If the offer is very relevant to what they initially opted in for you could just
send your whole list the entire launch. However, in general it’s preferred to
put them into a launch list because they’re much more powerful afterward.
!
